last_action: T005 complete — documented speckit.aiContextInstructions setting in README

T001.did: Added speckit.aiContextInstructions boolean config (default true) under contributes.configuration.properties

T002.did: Created buildPrompt helper that wraps commands with a marker-delimited preamble listing canonical substeps; returns raw command when config disabled or step unknown

T003.did: Added 7 unit tests covering all 4 known steps, opt-out, unknown step, omitted step, path-leakage, and token-budget (<1500 chars)

T004.did: Routed both executeInTerminal dispatch sites in specCommands.ts through buildPrompt; added toWorkspaceRelative helper so only workspace-relative specDir is embedded

T005.did: Added AI Context Instructions section to README between Command Format and Spec Directories

approach: Introduce a single promptBuilder helper and route every executeInTerminal caller through it, gated by a new speckit.aiContextInstructions setting.

specify.key_finding: Every AI provider implements executeInTerminal(prompt); there is no central prompt builder — each command in specCommands.ts assembles its own prompt string, so adding a wrapper helper is the clean integration point.

plan.approach_summary: Introduce a single promptBuilder helper and route every executeInTerminal caller through it, gated by a new speckit.aiContextInstructions setting.
risks: Token cost: +200–300 tokens per dispatch — accept per spec; revisit after launch if budget complaints surface.
AI non-compliance: Best-effort; spec 061 remains the hard guarantee for step-boundary writes.
Path leakage: Only workspace-relative specDir is embedded; unit test asserts no absolute path substrings.
